text: Continuous integration, or CI, is a development practice where team members regularly merge their code changes into a central repository. Every time new code is added, an automated build is triggered. This process compiles the code and runs a series of tests to check for errors. The goal is to catch issues early, before they become larger problems. These tasks are managed by a CI pipeline, which runs each step automatically in sequence. By using CI, developers receive immediate feedback on their code, helping teams maintain software quality and collaborate more effectively.
